knowledge_gaps: The 2006-07 FX regime is the most informative dollar-cycle analogue for predicting the current dollar and correlation trajectory.
Standard Financial Conditions Indices systematically mismeasure tightening under positive correlation regimes by treating stock and bond components as independent.
The 2006-07 FX regime is the most informative dollar-cycle analogue for current conditions based on seven-metric dimensional comparison
The NAIRU has shifted up 0.3-0.5pp to ~4.3-4.5% meaning current 4.1% unemployment is tighter than it appears
Petrodollar recycling erosion is a meaningful structural variable connecting dollar dynamics to the correlation regime through declining price-insensitive Treasury demand
Passive investing (~50% of equity AUM) extends the correlation-regime equity repricing lag from 18-36 months to 36-60 months and concentrates repricing into shorter, sharper events
Passive investing (~50% of equity AUM) extends the correlation-regime equity repricing lag from the historical 18-36 months to 36-60 months.
FCI miscalibration is specifically 75-150bp in policy-rate-equivalent terms due to AI equity rally effects
Sanctions-driven global correlation regime bifurcation is creating distinct US/EU, China, and non-aligned EM correlation blocs.
Portfolio construction under persistent positive correlation
The 1947-1951 financial repression period is an underappreciated analogue for the current stock-bond correlation regime.
Historical analogue analysis (1970s, 1990s)

The geopolitical correlation ratchet creates a structurally positive correlation bias through asymmetric escalation-spike vs. de-escalation-normalization dynamics and permanent sunk fiscal commitments.
Geopolitical fragmentation (defense spending, tech decoupling, reshoring) is a substantial independent driver of positive correlation beyond the standard inflation and fiscal channels
A Russia-Ukraine settlement is the most powerful single catalyst for returning to negative stock-bond correlation, with market pricing potentially underweighting this tail at ~15% probability.
The Taiwan contingency is the single largest underpriced tail risk for rates-equity correlation given 90% semiconductor disruption and potential Chinese Treasury liquidation (~$800B)

AI-driven supply-side productivity gains (50-100bp annual TFP growth) could be the most powerful structural force restoring negative correlation, overriding fiscal dominance regardless of fiscal policy or central bank reaction functions
The BOJ YCC abandonment provides a natural experiment suggesting transmission restoration initially promotes positive correlation with a 12-18 month lag
Financial Conditions Index miscalibration is 75-150bp in policy-rate-equivalent terms due to AI equity rally loosening conditions through a non-monetary channel.
Italy's BTP-FTSE MIB correlation trajectory provides a 12-18 month advanced preview of the US stock-bond correlation regime.
EUR/USD carry-adjusted appreciation functions as a 2-4 month leading indicator for stock-bond correlation regime shifts.
BOJ normalization is the single largest underpriced exogenous shock to US term premium and global correlation regimes, potentially adding 30-180bp to US term premium over 12-18 months.

AI-driven supply-side productivity gains could be the most powerful structural force restoring negative correlation, potentially overriding fiscal dominance regardless of fiscal policy or central bank reaction functions within 3-5 years.
Correlation regime acts as an earnings quality factor rotation signal, with high-accruals quintile performance flipping between positive and negative correlation regimes.
A Taiwan Strait crisis is the most underpriced geopolitical correlation risk, with options markets pricing Taiwan risk through equity vol but not rate vol or cross-asset correlation.
BOJ normalization would add 30-180bp to US term premium via $100-300B in Japanese selling pressure over 12-18 months, representing the single largest underpriced exogenous shock
Phillips Curve convexity near the zero output gap makes the current economy maximally sensitive to small shocks producing 0.15-0.25 correlation swings in rolling 3-month windows

Geopolitical escalation produces a ratchet effect biasing expected correlation permanently positive over geopolitical cycles through irreversible fiscal commitments
EUR/USD carry-adjusted appreciation functions as a 2-4 month leading indicator for correlation regime shifts based on three historical validations
Unstable oscillation may constitute a permanent third regime rather than a transition between positive and negative correlation regimes
Italy's BTP-FTSE MIB correlation trajectory provides a 12-18 month advanced preview of the US correlation regime based on chronological parallels
USD/JPY encodes global duration risk appetite and serves as a 1-3 month early-warning system for correlation regime shifts via carry unwind signaling
